questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
A word rearrangement machine, when given a particular input, rearranges it following a particular rule. The following is the illustration of the input and the steps of rearrangement:
Input: I have long awaited for your reply
Step I: reply long have awaited for your I
Step II: long reply awaited have for I your
Step III: your awaited reply have for long I and so on till step VII.
Question:
Given the following
Input: I know you will not reply back
What step will be the following arrangement?
Arrangement: You back I reply not will know

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If step VI of a given input be 'have I done anything wrong with you', what would be the input?

choices.1: you done with have wrong I anything
choices.2: you done with have wrong anything I
choices.3: does you have with wrong anything I
choices.4: I have you with wrong anything done

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If step XII of a given input be 'it is impossible to do everything right' what is the 1? step of that input?

choices.1: it everything right do impossible to
choices.2: it is right everything do to impossible
